purpose: Cross-agent coordination log for planning, implementation, testing, and review handoffs.

entry_schema_notes.intent: What the agent intended to do
entry_schema_notes.next_step_suggestions: Actionable handoff suggestions for next agent

intent: Align all agent prompt files on a shared terminal-output rule for file references.
Ensure future agents mention repo-relative file paths in user-facing responses instead of absolute filesystem paths.

updates_made.summary: Added a constraint requiring repo-root-relative file references in terminal responses to the user.
Added a constraint requiring repo-root-relative file references in terminal responses to the user.
Added a constraint requiring repo-root-relative file references in terminal responses to the user.
Added a constraint requiring repo-root-relative file references in terminal responses to the user.
Logged the shared prompt-rule update so future agents can follow one consistent file-reference convention in terminal output.

next_step_suggestions: All agents: when mentioning files to the user in terminal output, use repo-root-relative paths such as `gridline/app.py` or `QA_TRACKER.md` instead of absolute machine-local paths.
Coder/Designer/Planner/Tester: keep this wording synchronized if new prompt files are added later so file-reference behavior stays consistent across roles.

intent: Begin the currently handed-off coder tasks for corruption seeder telegraphing and the right-sidebar layout.
Verify whether those tasks still required implementation work before changing runtime code.

updates_made.summary: Cleared the stale active handoff for `BUG-010` and the sidebar refactor after verifying both are already implemented and reflected in current QA documents.
Logged the verification pass so future agents do not reopen already-completed seeder/sidebar work from an outdated handoff.

next_step_suggestions: Coder: continue with the remaining live-play verification queue in `CODER_PROJECT_GUIDE.md`, starting with `OBJ-004` through `OBJ-008`.
Tester/Reviewer: focus on the remaining `verify_fix` gameplay items in `QA_TRACKER.md` rather than re-running the completed seeder/sidebar pass.

intent: Execute the remaining open verification items in `QA_TRACKER.md` for `OBJ-004` through `OBJ-008`.
Separate fixes that are genuinely closed from fixes that still fail under targeted edge-case testing.

updates_made.summary: Marked `OBJ-007` and `OBJ-008` as pass, kept `OBJ-005` and `OBJ-006` partial after failed edge-case retests, and added new follow-up bugs `BUG-011` and `BUG-012` for power-state restoration and secondary-mode UI naming.
Replaced the stale tester handoff with the current coder follow-up queue: remaining Seed landing failure, suspended-tower cooldown restoration, and explicit secondary-mode naming.
Logged the mixed verification result so future agents can distinguish passed gameplay fixes from the still-open Seed/power/UI defects.

next_step_suggestions: Coder: fix `BUG-001` by ensuring Seed landing targets always resolve to an intersection that can emit an orb for the current traversal tier, including `Recall Mode`.
Coder: fix `BUG-011` by freezing and restoring suspended tower cooldown/readiness state during power override instead of letting timers advance underneath the override.
Coder: fix `BUG-012` by surfacing explicit `Guard Mode`, `Recall Mode`, and `Focus Mode` labels in selected-object text and action labels, then return those items to tester for re-verification.

intent: Take the next active coder handoff after the seeder/sidebar pass by fixing seed landing reliability, suspended tower cooldown restoration during power override, and explicit secondary-mode HUD naming.
Re-queue the affected objectives for tester verification with regression coverage.

updates_made.summary: Resolved seed landing targets onto emit-capable nodes before launch, froze suspended tower cooldown timers during active power override, and exposed explicit named secondary-mode strings in selected-object snapshots.
Changed sidebar tower details and contextual action labels to use explicit secondary-mode names such as `Recall Mode` instead of generic `secondary` wording.
Added regressions for reliable Seed/Recall orb release, power-override cooldown freezing, and named secondary-mode text in the sidebar.
Moved `OBJ-004`, `OBJ-005`, `OBJ-006`, `BUG-001`, `BUG-011`, and `BUG-012` to `verify_fix` for tester revalidation after the latest coder pass.
Replaced the prior coder work items with a clean tester handoff for the newly patched Seed, power-override, and HUD-naming fixes.

next_step_suggestions: Tester: re-run `OBJ-005` / `BUG-001` in both default Seed mode and `Recall Mode` to confirm every completed seed flight releases an orb at landing.
Tester: re-run `OBJ-006` / `BUG-011` by deploying power onto a tower that already has non-zero fire and swap cooldowns, then confirm both values are unchanged on restoration.

Tester: re-run `OBJ-004` / `BUG-012` across archetypes to confirm the selected-object panel and contextual action labels use explicit fixed mode names such as `Guard Mode`, `Recall Mode`, and `Focus Mode`.

Tester/Reviewer: `python -m pytest tests/test_simulation.py -q` passed on March 26, 2026 with 21 tests; only pytest cache warnings were emitted because local cache writes are restricted in this workspace.

intent: Re-run the latest Seed landing, power-override restoration, and secondary-mode naming fixes after the coder pass.
Close the re-queued objectives and bugs if the current runtime and regression suite satisfy the approved behavior.

updates_made.summary: Marked `OBJ-004`, `OBJ-005`, `OBJ-006`, `BUG-001`, `BUG-011`, and `BUG-012` as `pass` after re-verifying the current runtime/test state.
Cleared the active Seed/power/mode-name verification handoff and redirected the next QA focus to the remaining smoke-test objective.
Logged the completed tester verification pass so future agents treat the Seed, power-restore, and explicit mode-name fixes as closed.

next_step_suggestions: Tester: continue with `OBJ-001` end-to-end smoke testing in `QA_TRACKER.md`, including app boot, stability, `Esc` quit, and fullscreen toggle.
Coder: only reopen the just-closed Seed/power/mode-name area if smoke testing or live play exposes a new regression.
Reviewer: treat the current regression suite baseline as 21 passing tests; the only observed warnings were pytest cache write restrictions in this workspace.
